entities.name: pratibimba
entities.cite: A mountain shows in the facet of a jewel

entities.name: bhāva
entities.cite: entities really of like form with the mountain reflected

entities.name: abhāsavikāra
entities.cite: only the one word under different manifesting conditions

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: it is the original *as appearing under* an alien condition, and its size-in-the-mirror is a fact about the mirroring
there are no duplicates at all, only the one word under different manifesting conditions

axioms.name: na_dvitiya
axioms.cite: a reflection is not a second entity resembling the first

denials.name: satya_bhava
denials.cite: If reflections were real duplicates — entities sarupa with their originals — the facet would have to contain a mountain-sized thing, which is absurd

claims.name: na_dvitiya
claims.cite: there is no possibility of entities really of like form with the mountain reflected

claims.name: asraya
claims.cite: a gem-facet, a blade-surface and the like

rejected_readings.label: literal_duplicate_entity
rejected_readings.rendering: A mountain reflected in a mirror is a real entity with the same form as the original mountain.
rejected_readings.why: Treats the reflection as possessing satya-bhava (real entity status) and sarupa (same-form property), which the commentary explicitly refutes. The denial forbids treating reflections as real duplicates; this reading asserts exactly that contradiction.

rejected_readings.label: size_transfer_doctrine
rejected_readings.rendering: The size-in-the-mirror is a real property transferred from the original to the reflection.
rejected_readings.why: Proposes that parimana literally transfers from the original to the reflection as a real transferrable property. The commentary refutes this: size-in-the-mirror is a fact about the mirroring condition (abhasavikara), not a transferred property. This incorrectly sorts the mirrored-size as a property rather than an appearance-dependent manifestation.

notes: This verse provides the strongest reductio in the reflection-series because it uses quantitative transfer (the size contradiction in small mirrors) to demonstrate that reflections cannot be real duplicates. Applied to the word: the sphota appearing in quick or slow utterance is not a short or long duplicate but the same word under different manifestation conditions. The contract avoids committing to whether the reflection is completely illusory (a later Advaitic position) vs. a real appearance-dependent manifestation, capturing only the shared anti-duplication principle.
